topic: An artist's work is displayed without their permission
raw_output:
[story 1]

The gallery lights felt like physical spotlights, burning directly into my exposed skin. I stood near a towering canvas, my heart thumping a frantic rhythm against my ribs. My piece, the one I’d poured months of sleepless nights into, was hanging right next to a sprawling, abstract piece I didn't recognize. A curator, with an air of practiced nonchalance, was gesturing toward my work, his smile wide and utterly devoid of apology. I felt my cheeks flush a deep, undeniable crimson, and I instinctively hunched my shoulders, trying to shrink into the space between the wall and the velvet rope. Every glance felt like an interrogation; I could feel the weight of every patron’s scrutiny pressing down on my shoulders.

[story 2]

"This texture is simply breathtaking," a voice purred from across the room. I kept my gaze fixed on my shoes, focusing intently on the scuff marks on the polished floor. When the gallery owner finally turned to me, his expression was one of detached admiration, and I could feel a sudden, hot prickle rising up my neck. I clutched the strap of my portfolio so tightly my knuckles turned white. I wanted to melt into the marble pedestal beneath me. The silence in my head was deafening, filled only with the sound of my own shallow breathing as I desperately searched for an escape route, a way to disappear from this overwhelming spotlight.

[story 3]

I watched, frozen near the entrance, as people drifted in, their conversations hushed and reverent. Then, a murmur rippled through the crowd, and suddenly, every eye seemed to swivel toward my studio wall. My breath hitched. It wasn't just that my work was on display; it was the sheer audacity of it, the lack of any prior consultation. A wave of intense self-consciousness washed over me, making my hands tremble slightly as I adjusted my collar. I wanted to bolt, to dissolve into the shadows, but my feet felt rooted to the spot. I managed a small, tight nod to a passing acquaintance, my voice coming out sounding unnaturally high and strained.
